## 2026-06-06 - TAP Deal Room Checkout Session Status

### Scope
- Added `GET /api/tap/deal-room/checkout/session/{session_id}` for display-only Checkout Session lookup.
- Added a safe status payload that excludes customer email and customer details.
- Added `Verify status` to the success return notice, with restored button state and visible no-secret recovery feedback.
- Extended `tap_checkout_return_notice` browser smoke to click the status action and assert the structured 503 response when `STRIPE_SECRET_KEY` is missing.

### A/B Decision
- Baseline: the return notice exposed the Checkout Session ID but no operator status check.
- Variant: retrieve/summarize Checkout Session status when Stripe credentials are configured, and fail closed with a visible status line when they are not.
- External guidance: Stripe success-page docs recommend using the Checkout Session ID to look up the Checkout Session for success-page display; Stripe also warns that fulfillment must remain webhook-backed.
- Decision: adopted because focused tests, live browser smoke, direct MCP browser proof, and canonical getdaytrends smoke all covered the new path.

## 2026-06-06 - TAP Target Market Enter Key

### Scope
- Added an explicit Enter-key apply workflow to the TAP alert queue target-market input.
- Kept focus on `#tap-target-country` after applying a market.
- Extended browser smoke to verify `japan` and `united-states` both apply through Enter, update the queue status, and set the matching preset to `aria-pressed=true`.
- Hardened the Checkout Session status smoke wait loop after a fixed-delay timing issue surfaced during the expanded browser run.

### A/B Decision
- Baseline: operators could apply a typed target market by blur/change or by clicking Refresh queue, but Enter had no explicit apply contract.
- Variant: `handleTapTargetMarketKey(event)` handles Enter, prevents the default action, refreshes the preset strip, and runs `syncTapOpsView()`.
- External guidance: W3C WCAG 2.2 keyboard operability and focus-visible criteria support making the typed-market workflow fully keyboard-operable while preserving input focus.
- Decision: adopted because unit coverage, live browser smoke, direct MCP browser proof, and canonical getdaytrends smoke all cover the new path with no TAP regression.

## 2026-06-06 - Operator Action Button Descriptions

### Scope
- Added per-blocker `aria-describedby` context for repeated operator blocker action buttons.
- Kept visible button text and existing exact `aria-label` selectors stable for compatibility.
- Added `operator_action_buttons_described` to browser smoke and strict TAP fixture readiness requirements.

### A/B Decision
- Baseline: repeated blocker buttons had generic accessible labels such as `Copy remediation action`, `View recovery packet`, and `Copy recovery packet path`; visual grouping showed context, but assistive technology users had no programmatic relationship to the specific blocker.
- Variant: generate an ID for each blocker title and attach it through `aria-describedby` to the blocker action buttons.
- External guidance: WCAG 2.2 expects labels to describe purpose and UI component names/roles to be programmatically determinable; WAI-ARIA 1.2 defines `aria-describedby` for additional object descriptions.
- Decision: adopted because the variant improves assistive-tech context without changing visible labels, and the new named browser check is now required by readiness.

## 2026-06-06 - Checkout Session Status Live Region

### Scope
- Added `role="status" aria-live="polite" aria-atomic="true"` to the nested Checkout Session status line in the TAP checkout return notice.
- Extended `tap_checkout_return_notice` browser smoke to require the nested status-region contract before and after Verify status.

### A/B Decision
- Baseline: the checkout return notice itself was a status region, but the nested session-status line updated by Verify status only had `aria-live="polite"`.
- Variant: expose the nested line as a full status region with atomic update semantics.
- External guidance: WAI-ARIA 1.2 defines `status` as a live-region role with polite and atomic behavior, and WCAG 2.2 requires status messages to be programmatically determinable without moving focus.
- Decision: adopted because it improves async checkout recovery feedback while preserving the no-secret fail-closed behavior and existing checkout return controls.

### QC Checks
- `python -m py_compile dashboard_html.py scripts\browser_smoke.py tests\test_dashboard.py tests\test_browser_smoke.py`

## 2026-06-06 - Dashboard Chart Canvas Accessibility

### Scope
- Added accessible image semantics, descriptive labels, and fallback text to the five Chart.js dashboard canvases.
- Covered the timeline, category mix, source quality, daily LLM cost, and trend acceleration charts.
- Added `dashboard_chart_canvases_accessible` to browser smoke so future evidence must prove role/name/fallback coverage.

### A/B Decision
- Baseline: Chart.js canvases were visible, but the canvas nodes had no role, accessible name, or fallback text.
- Variant: render each chart canvas with `role="img"`, a specific `aria-label`, and matching fallback text.
- External guidance: Chart.js documentation puts canvas accessibility on the page author through ARIA attributes or fallback content; MDN documents that canvas pixels are not exposed to accessibility tools as semantic HTML.
- Decision: adopted because it improves non-text chart alternatives without changing Chart.js IDs or dashboard layout, and the new named browser check is covered by TAP fixture/default browser evidence.

## 2026-06-06 - Manual Copy Dialog Hidden State

### Scope
- Added an explicit HTML `hidden` contract to the operator manual-copy fallback dialog.
- Kept the existing forced clipboard-failure flow, Escape handling, Close button behavior, textarea selection, and focus restoration.
- Strengthened `operator_copy_failure_feedback` so browser smoke proves initial, open, Escape-close, and Close-button hidden states.

### A/B Decision
- Baseline: the manual-copy panel was visually hidden with CSS and `.show`, but the smoke path did not assert the DOM `hidden` state.
- Variant: render the closed dialog with `hidden`, toggle `panel.hidden` in `showManualCopy` and `hideManualCopy`, and clear any stale `aria-hidden` so visible focusable content is never hidden from assistive technology.
- External guidance: MDN documents `hidden` as the right state for content not currently presented, notes that `aria-hidden` is redundant when `hidden` is present, and WAI-ARIA APG expects dialog focus entry plus Escape close behavior.
- Decision: adopted because the variant improves accessibility state fidelity without changing the operator copy workflow or visible UI.

### QC Checks
- `python -m py_compile dashboard_html.py scripts\browser_smoke.py tests\test_dashboard.py tests\test_browser_smoke.py`
  - Result: PASS.

  - Result: `45/45 PASS`.

## 2026-06-06 - Trends Table Caption

### Scope
- Added a visually hidden caption to the latest scored trends table.
- Kept the table visual layout, column headers, and `tbody#t-body` JavaScript target unchanged.
- Added `dashboard_trends_table_accessible` to browser smoke.

### A/B Decision
- Baseline: the trends table had headers but no table-level caption/name.
- Variant: add `<caption class="visually-hidden">Latest scored trends</caption>` as the first child of the table and verify caption/header/body structure in browser smoke.
- External guidance: MDN documents `<caption>` as providing a table accessible name/description and W3C WAI guidance recommends captions to identify tables.
- Decision: adopted because it improves table identification for assistive technology users without adding visible dashboard noise.

## 2026-06-06 - Trends Table Header Scope

### Scope
- Added explicit `scope="col"` to all seven latest scored trends table headers.
- Strengthened `dashboard_trends_table_accessible` to capture and require column scopes.
- Kept caption placement, header order, and `tbody#t-body` unchanged.

### A/B Decision
- Baseline: the trends table had a caption and header cells, but header-to-column association relied on browser inference.
- Variant: add `scope="col"` to every table header and prove the values through browser smoke.
- External guidance: MDN documents `scope` as defining which cells a `<th>` relates to, and W3C WAI guidance recommends `scope="col"` to associate header cells with corresponding data cells in larger or ambiguous tables.
- Decision: adopted because it improves assistive technology semantics without changing the visible dashboard or data render target.

- Baseline: the dashboard exposed content sections but did not provide a semantic `main` landmark or skip target.
- Variant: make the skip link the first body child, add a single focusable `main#dashboard-main`, and verify both structure and keyboard activation in browser smoke.
- External guidance: MDN documents `<main>` as the main landmark and skip-link target, while W3C WAI WCAG 2.4.1 and Technique G1 describe bypass links that move focus to main content.
- Decision: adopted because it improves keyboard and assistive-technology navigation without changing the visible operator workflow.

## 2026-06-06 - Dashboard Status Pill Live Region

### Scope
- Added a programmatic status-message contract to the visible `#status-pill`.
- Kept the existing `loadPipeline()` and error fallback as the single source of visible status text.
- Added `dashboard_status_pill_live_region` to browser smoke.

### A/B Decision
- Baseline: the status pill showed `Loading`, `Idle`, `Running`, or `Error`, but had no role or live-region attributes.
- Variant: add `role="status"`, `aria-live="polite"`, `aria-atomic="true"`, and `aria-label="Dashboard status"` to the existing visible pill, then verify it remains visible, non-focusable, and unfocused after page load.
- External guidance: MDN and W3C WAI ARIA22 describe `role=status` as the right live region for non-interruptive application status updates, and WCAG 4.1.3 expects status messages to be programmatically determinable without moving focus.
- Decision: adopted because it improves assistive-technology parity for the top-level operator state without changing layout, polling, or click workflows.

## 2026-06-06 - Dashboard Footer Landmark

### Scope
- Converted the visual footer from `div.footer` to a semantic body-level `<footer class="footer">`.
- Kept the footer text and styling class unchanged.
- Added `dashboard_footer_contentinfo_landmark` to browser smoke.

### A/B Decision
- Baseline: the footer was visually present but semantically generic.
- Variant: use `<footer>` without redundant `role="contentinfo"`, keep it outside `main#dashboard-main`, and prove there is no legacy `div.footer`.
- External guidance: W3C H101 and WAI page-region guidance identify body-level `<footer>` as the semantic page footer/contentinfo region, while MDN documents that `<footer>` has implicit `contentinfo` semantics unless nested in sectioning content.
- Decision: adopted because it improves landmark navigation without changing dashboard layout, text, or operator workflows.

## 2026-06-06 - Workspace Smoke Failure Detail Preview

### Scope
- Added bounded failure details to the dashboard `Workspace smoke` disclosure preview.
- The expanded preview now includes `Detail`, `Command`, and `Output` for failed workspace-smoke checks.
- Commands render in `<code>` and output tails render in `<samp>`, with the existing disclosure button label and `aria-expanded`/`aria-controls` behavior preserved.
- The API masks PostgreSQL URLs, `tenant/user` values, OpenAI-style keys, Google API-style keys, and mojibake-prone log lines before returning failure detail.

### A/B Decision
- Baseline: clicking `View workspace smoke` showed status, provenance, summary, and failed check names, but not the actual command or output that explained the failure.
- Variant: expose a bounded `failed_details` array from `/api/operator/workspace-smoke` and render `Detail`, `Command`, and `Output` rows in the existing preview.
- External guidance: MDN documents `<samp>` for computer-program output, and W3C APG disclosure guidance supports keeping a concise disclosure control with richer detail in the controlled panel.
- Decision: adopted because it improves operator diagnosis speed without changing readiness logic, artifact selection, or launch-gate semantics.

### QC Checks
- `python -m py_compile dashboard.py dashboard_html.py scripts\browser_smoke.py tests\test_dashboard.py tests\test_browser_smoke.py`
  - Result: PASS.
- `python -m pytest tests/test_dashboard.py tests/test_browser_smoke.py -q`
  - Result: `98 passed`.
- `python scripts\browser_smoke.py --report logs\smoke\dashboard_browser_workspace_smoke_failure_detail_2026-06-06.json --screenshot logs\smoke\dashboard_browser_workspace_smoke_failure_detail_2026-06-06.png`

## 2026-06-07 - Readiness Verification Bundle Working Directory

### Scope
- Added a dynamic PowerShell working-directory bootstrap line to the readiness verification copy bundle.
- `/api/operator/readiness-report` now returns `verification_shell`, `verification_working_directory`, and a `verification_command_bundle` that starts with `Set-Location -LiteralPath`.
- The readiness report preview now shows `Verification cwd:` so operators can confirm the command context before copying.
- Browser smoke now verifies the copied bundle starts in the project cwd, matches clipboard contents exactly, includes required verification commands, and has no raw secret-shaped values.

### A/B Decision
- Baseline: `Copy verify` copied the right command sequence but assumed the operator was already in `automation\getdaytrends`.
- Variant: prepend `Set-Location -LiteralPath '<getdaytrends cwd>'` and verify it in the actual clipboard payload.
- External guidance: Microsoft Learn documents `Set-Location` for setting the working location, MDN Clipboard `writeText()` supports async text copy, and W3C APG button guidance matches native button command actions.
- Decision: adopted because it makes the post-fix verification bundle paste-safe in PowerShell without changing readiness scoring or strict launch blockers.

- `python scripts\browser_smoke.py`

## 2026-06-07 - Direct Recovery Bundle Copy

### Scope
- Added a row-level `Copy recovery bundle` button to each operator recovery packet action.
- The button fetches the selected `/api/operator/recovery-packet?path=...` payload and copies `recovery_bundle` without opening the packet preview.
- Browser smoke now verifies both Supabase and provider row-level bundle copy paths, including clipboard payload fragments and secret-shaped value guards.

### A/B Decision
- Baseline: copying the complete recovery bundle required opening `View recovery packet` and then clicking `Copy all`.
- Variant: keep the preview workflow, but add direct row-level bundle copy for faster external credential-repair handoff.
- External guidance: MDN Clipboard `writeText()` supports async text copy, W3C APG button guidance matches command actions as native buttons, and Microsoft `Set-Location -LiteralPath` supports paste-safe PowerShell workdir setup.
- Decision: adopted because the variant removes a recovery handoff step without changing readiness scoring, recovery packet generation, or launch blocker semantics.

  - Result: `53/53 PASS`, including `operator_recovery_bundle_row_copy` and `operator_provider_recovery_bundle_row_copy`.

  - Result: `58/58 PASS`.

## 2026-06-07 - Clipboard Permission Fail Closed

### Scope
- Changed `copyOperatorText()` so async Clipboard API denial fails closed instead of trusting `document.execCommand('copy')`.
- `document.execCommand('copy')` remains available only when `navigator.clipboard.writeText` is unavailable.
- Added browser-smoke coverage for `writeText()` denied plus `execCommand=true` to prove the UI shows manual-copy instead of false success.

### A/B Decision
- Baseline: direct MCP click testing showed a false-success case where `data-copy-result=copied` was shown while the readable clipboard still contained unrelated prior text.
- Variant: if modern async clipboard exists and rejects, mark copy failed, show the manual-copy panel, and keep error toast semantics.
- External guidance: MDN Clipboard `writeText()` documents the async clipboard write path; W3C APG button guidance supports keeping command actions as native buttons with explicit labels.
- Decision: adopted because fail-closed copy status is safer for recovery commands than a possibly false successful state.

### QC Checks
- `python -m py_compile dashboard_html.py scripts\browser_smoke.py tests\test_dashboard.py tests\test_browser_smoke.py`
  - Result: PASS.
- `python -m pytest tests/test_dashboard.py tests/test_browser_smoke.py -q`
  - Result: `101 passed`.
- `python scripts\browser_smoke.py --report logs\smoke\dashboard_browser_clipboard_fail_closed_2026-06-07.json --screenshot logs\smoke\dashboard_browser_clipboard_fail_closed_2026-06-07.png`
  - Result: `54/54 PASS`, including `operator_copy_async_clipboard_denied_fail_closed`.
- `python scripts\browser_smoke.py`
  - Result: `54/54 PASS`; refreshed `logs\smoke\dashboard_browser_latest.json`.
- `python scripts\browser_smoke.py --tap-source-fixture --report logs\smoke\tap_fixture_browser_latest.json --screenshot logs\smoke\tap_fixture_browser_latest.png`
  - Result: `59/59 PASS`.
- Direct MCP browser check against `http://127.0.0.1:8069/`
  - Injected `navigator.clipboard.writeText` denial and `document.execCommand=true`.
  - Clicked `Copy remediation action`.
  - Result: `copyResult=failed`, `role=alert`, `aria-live=assertive`, manual-copy panel visible, manual-copy text length `163`, and `execCommandCalled=false`.
  - Browser state was restored; server on port `8069` was stopped and the port was `not_listening`.
- `python scripts\readiness_check.py --max-scheduler-age-hours 24 --max-cli-smoke-age-hours 24 --max-browser-smoke-age-hours 24 --fail-on-runtime-fallback --require-live-db`
  - Result: expected exit `1`; remaining blockers are `cli_smoke_report`, `provider_auth_report`, and `live_db_doctor`.
- `python ops\scripts\run_workspace_smoke.py --scope getdaytrends --json-out var\workspace-smoke-getdaytrends-clipboard-fail-closed-2026-06-07.json`
  - Result: expected exit `1`; `5/6 PASS`, only `getdaytrends launch readiness gate` failed.
  - Full project test subcheck: `972 passed, 7 skipped, 1 warning`.
- `python scripts\check_text_hygiene.py`
  - Result: PASS, `0` findings.

## 2026-06-07 - Readiness Report Preview

### Scope
- Added `/api/operator/readiness-report` as a sanitized preview endpoint for the latest readiness JSON.
- Added a `View readiness report` disclosure next to the existing readiness report copy action.
- The preview shows status, generation time, artifact path, summary, failed checks, blocker messages, actions, and bounded diagnostics.
- Browser smoke now verifies open/close disclosure state and raw secret masking for the readiness report preview.

### A/B Decision
- Baseline: `Readiness report` was copy-only and required opening `readiness_latest.json` outside the app.
- Variant: render a bounded in-app preview using the existing `View`/`Hide` disclosure pattern.
- External guidance: MDN documents disclosure UI with `<details>`, while W3C APG disclosure guidance supports a button that expands and collapses controlled content. The local dashboard already uses that button pattern for recovery and workspace smoke previews.
- Decision: adopted because it improves operator diagnosis speed without changing readiness scoring or suppressing the strict launch gate.

## 2026-06-06 - Fallback Banner Endpoint Details

### Scope
- Added endpoint-level detail to the dashboard fallback-mode banner.
- The banner now keeps a short live status summary in `dashboard-warning-status` and exposes degraded endpoint rows in a native `<details>` disclosure.
- Each row includes panel label, API path, degraded source, and reason.

### A/B Decision
- Baseline: fallback mode showed panel chips but not the endpoint path or degraded source/reason.
- Variant: track degradation metadata from `x-dashboard-degraded-source`, `x-dashboard-degraded-reason`, and `_meta`, then render a compact details disclosure.
- External guidance: MDN documents native `<details>`/`<summary>` for disclosures; W3C ARIA22 and MDN status-role guidance support a polite, atomic status child that does not move focus.
- Decision: adopted because it improves fallback diagnosis without changing endpoint behavior, fallback payloads, or launch-gate semantics.

## 2026-06-07 - Readiness Artifact Copy Actions

### Scope

- `/api/operator/readiness` now exposes `readiness_refresh_command` using the existing strict refresh command.
- Browser smoke now validates both copy paths: the report path must include `readiness_latest.json`, and the strict command must include the browser freshness, runtime fallback, and live DB gates.

- Baseline: the operator panel exposed workspace smoke evidence but not the canonical readiness report path or strict rerun command.
- Variant: render readiness report and refresh command actions next to workspace smoke, reusing the existing `copyOperatorAction` workflow.

## 2026-06-07 - Recovery Success Criteria Row Copy

### Scope
- Added row-level `Copy success` actions for operator recovery packets.
- The action fetches the selected recovery packet and copies a concise `Recovery success criteria:` list from `launch_success_criteria`.
- Browser smoke now validates Supabase and provider success-criteria copy paths, including expected criteria content, no markdown sections, and no raw secret-shaped values.

### A/B Decision
- Baseline: success criteria were available in recovery packet payloads and previews, but row-level actions only copied the full bundle or verification commands.
- Variant: add a row-level success-criteria copy action next to `Copy bundle` and `Copy verify`.
- External guidance: MDN Clipboard `writeText()` supports async text copy, and W3C APG button guidance matches using actual buttons for command actions.
- Decision: adopted because it gives operators a smaller post-repair acceptance checklist without changing recovery packet generation, preview rendering, readiness scoring, or strict launch blockers.

## 2026-06-07 - Artifact Action Manifest

### Scope
- Replaced hard-coded operator artifact row assembly with backend-provided `artifact_actions` metadata.
- Kept existing `artifacts` payload keys and added a frontend fallback renderer for compatibility.
- Limited frontend dispatch to known safe view kinds: `readiness_report`, `artifact_image`, and `workspace_smoke`.
- Added browser-smoke coverage for both the API manifest and the DOM rows rendered from `data-artifact-key`.

### A/B Decision
- Baseline: operator artifact rows were assembled with branch-heavy frontend HTML tied directly to specific artifact keys.
- Variant: the backend emits structured action metadata, while the frontend generically renders labels, copy controls, preview containers, and only whitelisted view handlers.
- External guidance: MDN documents `data-*` attributes and JavaScript `dataset` access for element-associated data; W3C APG button guidance supports native labeled buttons for command actions.
- Decision: adopted because the variant keeps old `artifacts` compatibility, reduces frontend branching, and prevents backend metadata from injecting arbitrary handlers.

## 2026-06-07 - Screenshot Error Text

### Scope
- Removed stale `Screenshot unavailable` text from successful TAP fixture screenshot previews.
- Kept the no-path fallback message unchanged.
- Changed the image error row to insert its text only after the image emits an `error` event.
- Strengthened browser smoke so a successfully loaded screenshot preview fails if unavailable text remains in the visible preview state.

### A/B Decision
- Baseline: latest app-click evidence showed `image_loaded=true` and `errorVisible=false`, but the preview text still contained `Screenshot unavailable`.
- Variant: keep the hidden error node empty until an actual image error occurs, then populate and reveal it.
- Primary KPI: loaded screenshot preview text must not contain `Screenshot unavailable`.
- Guardrails: image alt, dimensions, same-origin image endpoint, clipboard copy, and canonical browser smoke must remain green.
- External guidance: MDN documents hidden content as not currently relevant to the page; W3C WAI image guidance emphasizes useful image text alternatives.
- Decision: adopted because the variant removed stale operator noise without weakening the actual image-error fallback path.

## 2026-06-07 - Workspace Smoke Conclusion

### Scope
- Added a derived `conclusion` field to `/api/operator/workspace-smoke`.
- Preserved the raw workspace smoke lifecycle `status` field.
- Updated the operator preview to show the result conclusion first, then the raw run status as provenance.
- Strengthened browser smoke so failed workspace-smoke evidence must render `Workspace smoke: failure` while still showing `Run status: complete`.

### A/B Decision
- Baseline: workspace smoke preview could start with `Workspace smoke: complete` even when `summary.failed=1`.
- Variant: compute a result conclusion from summary/error state and render `Workspace smoke: failure` with `Run status: complete` below it.
- Primary KPI: failed workspace-smoke artifacts must be visually labeled as failure on the first preview line.
- Guardrails: raw lifecycle status remains visible, failed details remain available, secret masking remains intact, and canonical browser smoke stays green.
- External guidance: GitHub Check Runs separate lifecycle `status` from final `conclusion`; completed runs can still have conclusions such as `success` or `failure`.
- Decision: adopted because the variant makes the operator state less ambiguous without changing the underlying workspace-smoke artifact contract.

- `python -m py_compile dashboard.py dashboard_html.py scripts\browser_smoke.py tests\test_dashboard.py tests\test_browser_smoke.py`
  - Result: PASS.

  - Result: `106 passed`.

  - Result: `62/62 PASS`.
  - `operator_workspace_smoke_view` detail started with `Workspace smoke: failure` and included `Run status: complete`.
- `python scripts\browser_smoke.py`
  - Result: `62/62 PASS`; refreshed `logs\smoke\dashboard_browser_latest.json`.
- `python scripts\browser_smoke.py --tap-source-fixture --timeout 45`
  - Result: `67/67 PASS`; refreshed `logs\smoke\dashboard_browser_tap_source_evidence.json` and `.png`.

  - Result: clicked `View workspace smoke`.
  - Preview first line was `Workspace smoke: failure`.
  - Preview also showed `Run status: complete`, `Summary: 5/6 passed, 1 failed`, and `Failed checks: getdaytrends launch readiness gate`.

## 2026-06-07 - Degraded Endpoint Copy

### Scope
- Added `Copy degraded endpoint details` to the fallback data warning banner.
- Added a structured degraded endpoint bundle with label, path, source, and reason.
- Extended browser smoke coverage so `dashboard_fallback_banner_details` verifies the copy button, clipboard payload, CRLF/LF normalization, and status live-region guardrails.
- Fixed a direct-click-discovered HTML-template escaping regression by rendering `join('\\n')` in generated JavaScript.
- Prevented stale TAP fallback URLs from accumulating by clearing previous `/api/tap/` degradations before each `syncTapOpsView()` refresh.
- Hardened the TAP preset browser smoke path by waiting for the input value and using the DOM click path for the save-state branch after headless physical clicking proved flaky while direct browser logic was healthy.

### A/B Decision
- Baseline: `View degraded endpoint details` listed fallback endpoints, but operators could not copy the current degraded endpoint bundle in one action.
- Variant: add a compact copy action in the fallback banner and verify the copied payload includes all degraded endpoint entries, including entries beyond the visible 12-row preview.
- Primary KPI: browser smoke must pass `dashboard_fallback_banner_details` with a copied payload containing `/api/` paths and `reason` fields.
- Guardrails: no secret-bearing DB/API tokens in warning text, fallback banner remains a polite status region, and readiness/TAP/operator artifact checks stay green.
- External guidance: MDN documents async clipboard writes as promise-based clipboard updates, and MDN/W3C status-role guidance supports polite advisory live regions.
- Decision: adopted after direct app clicking proved the operator flow and browser smoke caught the Windows CRLF clipboard normalization edge.

### QC Checks
- `python -m py_compile dashboard_html.py scripts\browser_smoke.py tests\test_dashboard.py tests\test_browser_smoke.py`
  - Result: PASS.
- `python -m pytest tests/test_dashboard.py tests/test_browser_smoke.py -q`
  - Result: `109 passed`.
- `python scripts\browser_smoke.py --timeout 45 --report logs\smoke\dashboard_browser_degraded_endpoint_copy_2026-06-07.json --screenshot logs\smoke\dashboard_browser_degraded_endpoint_copy_2026-06-07.png`
  - Result: PASS, including `dashboard_fallback_banner_details`.
  - Confirmed `copyLineCount=12` and `rowCount=12`.
- `python scripts\browser_smoke.py`
  - Result: PASS; refreshed `logs\smoke\dashboard_browser_latest.json` and `.png`.
- `python scripts\browser_smoke.py --tap-source-fixture --timeout 45`
  - Result: PASS; refreshed `logs\smoke\dashboard_browser_tap_source_evidence.json` and `.png`.
- Direct MCP browser check against `http://127.0.0.1:8084/`
  - Result: opened `View degraded endpoint details`, clicked `Copy degraded endpoint details`, and confirmed `data-copy-result=copied`.
  - Copied payload started with `Fallback data mode:` and included `/api/` paths plus `reason dependency_unavailable`.
  - First reload exposed a JS parse issue from an unescaped template newline; fixed and reverified.
  - Follow-up smoke inspection confirmed stale TAP target/lifecycle endpoint variants no longer accumulate in the copied degraded endpoint bundle.

## 2026-06-07 - Browser Artifact Actions

- Baseline: readiness showed dashboard browser evidence, but `dashboard_browser_latest.json` and `dashboard_browser_latest.png` were not first-class operator artifact rows.
- Variant: expose both files next to readiness, TAP fixture, scheduler, and workspace-smoke artifacts.
- Primary KPI: browser smoke must pass `operator_dashboard_browser_report_copy` and `operator_dashboard_browser_screenshot_view`.
- Guardrails: TAP fixture evidence remains green, image artifact paths stay safe-local only, copy feedback remains fail-closed, and strict readiness keeps known external blockers visible.
- External guidance: GitHub Actions documents retained artifacts as debugging evidence, and Playwright documents saved screenshots as test/debug files.
- Decision: adopted because the variant makes the main browser evidence inspectable without leaving the dashboard and direct app testing found a real image-preview loading issue.

## 2026-06-07 - Scheduler Artifact Action

### Scope
- Added `Scheduler artifact` to the operator artifact action manifest.
- Added browser smoke coverage for copying the scheduler artifact path.
- Hardened workspace-smoke artifact selection so completed reports outrank newer partial reports.
- Pinned `View workspace smoke` to the artifact row's path so copy/view actions cannot drift during concurrent smoke generation.

### A/B Decision
- Baseline: the operator dashboard showed `Scheduler age`, but did not expose the scheduler JSON artifact as a copyable action.
- Variant A: add a copy-only `Scheduler artifact` row.
- Variant B: keep workspace-smoke previews bound to the row artifact path and prefer completed reports over partial reports.
- Primary KPI: browser smoke must pass `operator_scheduler_artifact_copy` and `operator_workspace_smoke_view`.
- Guardrails: direct app click must have no console errors, TAP fixture smoke must remain green, and strict readiness must keep known external blockers visible.
- External guidance: GitHub Actions documents test/build artifacts as debugging evidence, and GitHub status checks separate in-progress status from completed conclusions.
- Decision: adopted after canonical smoke exposed a real workspace-smoke preview race that Variant B fixed.

## 2026-06-07 - Workspace Smoke Recency

### Scope
- Changed `_latest_getdaytrends_workspace_smoke()` to select the latest valid workspace-smoke report by each artifact's `generated_at` timestamp first.
- Kept filesystem `st_mtime` as a fallback for older or timestamp-less reports.
- Added a regression test proving a newer generated report wins even when an older generated report is written or touched later.

### A/B Decision
- Baseline: dashboard workspace-smoke selection sorted `workspace-smoke-getdaytrends*.json` files by filesystem mtime.
- Variant: parse `generated_at` from each valid report and compare that logical report time before falling back to mtime.
- Primary KPI: a newer generated workspace-smoke artifact must be selected even if a stale report has a newer filesystem mtime.
- Guardrails: invalid JSON remains skipped, timestamp-less reports remain eligible through mtime fallback, and operator preview copy/view flows stay green.
- External guidance: Python `Path.stat().st_mtime` is filesystem metadata, while `datetime.fromisoformat()` parses the ISO timestamp already carried by workspace-smoke artifacts.
- Decision: adopted because the operator dashboard should reflect evidence recency, not incidental filesystem touch order.

### QC Checks
- `python -m py_compile dashboard.py tests\test_dashboard.py`
  - Result: PASS.
- `python -m pytest tests/test_dashboard.py tests/test_browser_smoke.py -q`
  - Result: `107 passed`.
- `python scripts\browser_smoke.py --timeout 45 --report logs\smoke\dashboard_browser_workspace_recency_2026-06-07.json --screenshot logs\smoke\dashboard_browser_workspace_recency_2026-06-07.png`

  - `operator_workspace_smoke_view` selected `D:\AI project\var\workspace-smoke-getdaytrends-workspace-conclusion-2026-06-07.json` with `Generated: 2026-06-06T19:19:12.732454+00:00`.

  - Result: `67/67 PASS`; refreshed `logs\smoke\dashboard_browser_tap_source_evidence.json` and `.png`.

  - Result: `#operator-artifacts` rendered workspace smoke path `D:\AI project\var\workspace-smoke-getdaytrends-workspace-conclusion-2026-06-07.json`.

## 2026-06-07 - Mobile Layout Guard

### Scope
- Fixed loaded mobile dashboard overflow by allowing grid children and panels to shrink, constraining charts to their panels, wrapping operator action controls, and wrapping long pipeline log lines.
- Strengthened `mobile_layout_no_page_overflow` so browser smoke waits for loaded operator rows before measuring page overflow and action-button clipping.
- Aligned readiness remediation text with the safe credential-update utility names so visible blocker rows, copied next actions, and recovery packets point to the same dry-run/write workflow.

### A/B Decision
- Baseline: direct mobile inspection showed `docScrollWidth` exceeded the viewport after loaded panels rendered, while the smoke guard could pass too early.
- Variant: apply responsive shrink/wrap CSS and verify loaded mobile state with `overflowPx`, offender count, action-button issue count, and recovery action button count.
- External guidance: MDN documents `flex-wrap` for allowing flex items to wrap onto multiple lines, and W3C APG button guidance matches keeping these as native command buttons.
- Decision: adopted because the variant fixed a real mobile layout defect without hiding the intentionally scrollable trends table or changing recovery/readiness semantics.

## 2026-06-07 - TAP Fixture Canonical Output

### Scope
- Added fixture-aware output path resolution to `scripts\browser_smoke.py`.
- `python scripts\browser_smoke.py --tap-source-fixture` now defaults to the strict-readiness canonical TAP fixture report and screenshot paths.
- Preserved explicit custom `--report` and `--screenshot` overrides, and preserved normal dashboard smoke defaults for non-fixture runs.

### A/B Decision
- Baseline: recent manual commands refreshed `tap_fixture_browser_latest.json`, while strict readiness reads `dashboard_browser_tap_source_evidence.json`, allowing the operator TAP fixture card to show older evidence.
- Variant: use `None` argparse defaults and resolve paths after parsing based on `--tap-source-fixture`.
- External guidance: Python argparse documents that optional argument defaults apply when an option string is absent, which lets the CLI distinguish omitted output paths from explicit overrides.
- Decision: adopted because it removes split TAP fixture evidence without breaking custom one-off smoke reports.

- Extended the Supabase packet verifier, browser smoke, and focused tests so this Windows-specific guidance is required in recovery-bundle copy and dedicated credential-command copy paths.
- Preserved the existing safe updater commands and secret masking.

- Baseline: direct MCP click on `http://127.0.0.1:8141/` showed `Copy credential update commands`, but the payload only said `send EOF` and did not mention `Ctrl+Z`.
- Variant: keep `send EOF` and add the explicit Windows key sequence from Python command-line documentation.
- Decision: adopted because the getdaytrends operator path is Windows + PowerShell and the copied command is a Python stdin workflow.

## 2026-06-07 - Log Viewer Mojibake Guard

### Scope
- Added a server-side guard for repeated `?` plus Hangul/Jamo mojibake in
  dashboard log lines.
- Added `dashboard_log_viewer_has_no_mojibake_markers` to browser smoke so
  visible `#log-viewer` corruption fails the run directly.
- Fixed the stale workspace smoke artifact selector uncovered by the first
  browser run; `launch-final` no longer overrides newer complete artifacts.

### A/B Decision
- Baseline: marker-only sanitizer and browser smoke checks let question-mark
  plus Hangul/Jamo mojibake render in the log viewer.
- Variant: use a Unicode-range regex to hide this unreadable pattern behind the
  existing log encoding placeholder and assert the rendered log viewer has no
  visible markers.
- External guidance: W3C character-encoding guidance treats unreadable and
  question-mark output as an encoding failure, and Python regex supports
  compiled Unicode string patterns for this detector.
- Decision: adopted because operators should see actionable log content, not
  unreadable corrupted text.

### QC Checks
- `python -m py_compile dashboard.py scripts\browser_smoke.py tests\test_dashboard.py tests\test_browser_smoke.py`

- `python -m pytest tests/test_dashboard.py -k "logs_endpoint_sanitizes" -q`

  - Result: `116 passed`.
- `python scripts\browser_smoke.py --port 8168 --timeout 45 --report logs\smoke\dashboard_browser_log_viewer_mojibake_guard_2026-06-07.json --screenshot logs\smoke\dashboard_browser_log_viewer_mojibake_guard_2026-06-07.png`

## 2026-06-07 - Credential Input Status Preflight

### Scope
- Added `python ..\..\ops\scripts\getdaytrends_update_credentials.py --input-status` to the Supabase credential update command bundle.
- Required the same preflight in Supabase recovery packet verification and browser smoke copied-bundle checks.
- Preserved the existing Windows clipboard dry-run/write path and interactive Ctrl+Z fallback commands.

### A/B Decision
- Baseline: the recovery bundle started at `--database-url-stdin`, so operators could rerun strict readiness even when no replacement credential inputs were staged.
- Variant: run `--input-status` first so the bundle reports whether process/User/Machine env vars, Credential Manager entries, or local Supabase management capability exist.
- Current probe result: `status=unchanged`, no `GETDAYTRENDS_NEW_*` process/user/machine env vars, zero matching Credential Manager targets, no Supabase CLI, and `safe_to_skip_strict_readiness_until_credential_inputs_change=true`.
- Decision: adopted because it avoids noisy strict reruns until external provider-console credential repair has produced a local input signal.

- Baseline: keep credential input status as a command-only preflight in the recovery bundle.

## 2026-06-07 - Clipboard Pipe Credential Update Commands

### Scope
- Added a PowerShell clipboard-pipe fast path to the Supabase recovery packet credential update command bundle.
- The copied command bundle now starts with `Get-Clipboard -Raw | python ..\..\ops\scripts\getdaytrends_update_credentials.py --database-url-stdin` for dry-run and the matching `--write` command for apply.
- Kept the interactive `Ctrl+Z, then Enter in PowerShell` fallback below the fast path.

### A/B Decision
- Baseline: direct operator copy flow had explicit Windows EOF guidance but still required interactive paste and EOF.
- Variant: add non-interactive `Get-Clipboard -Raw` dry-run/write commands while preserving the interactive fallback.
- External guidance: Microsoft PowerShell documents `Get-Clipboard -Raw` and pipeline behavior, supporting this local stdin workflow.
- Decision: adopted because the operator can copy the Supabase Transaction pooler URL once and avoid manual EOF while keeping the same safe updater script and secret masking.

- External guidance: GitHub Actions supports rerunning failed jobs, and PowerShell documents `&` as the call operator for invoking quoted command paths.

## 2026-06-07 - Artifact Freshness Notes

### Scope
- Added freshness notes to operator artifact rows in `/api/operator/readiness`.
- Reused existing readiness/browser/TAP/scheduler/workspace evidence timestamps instead of adding new scans.
- Rendered note badges beside artifact labels while preserving copy and preview controls.

### A/B Decision
- Baseline: direct MCP check on `http://127.0.0.1:8149/` showed artifact rows with `noteCount: 0`; generated time was hidden behind previews.
- Variant: expose per-row notes such as `fresh <0.1h old`, `fresh 10.6h old`, and `fresh 21.0h old`.
- External guidance: GitHub Actions artifact API exposes time metadata such as created/updated/expiry timestamps, and `gh run download` defaults to the latest artifact.
- Decision: adopted because the operator can now scan freshness without opening every artifact preview.

### QC Checks
- Direct MCP browser check against `http://127.0.0.1:8150/`
  - Result: artifact rows rendered 8 freshness notes and kept command-only rows note-free.
  - `View workspace smoke` opened the preview and preserved `Copy failed command`.

## 2026-06-07 - Scheduler Near-Stale Warning

### Scope
- Added a near-stale scheduler freshness warning for operator readiness when the latest scheduler artifact is between 90% and 100% of the 24-hour freshness limit.
- Kept readiness freshness state as `fresh` until the scheduler artifact actually exceeds 24 hours.
- Updated the operator `Scheduler age` card to render `warn` with refresh guidance before evidence expires.
- Added browser smoke coverage for the near-stale card and a manifest guard that rejects `workspace-smoke-getdaytrends-launch-final.json` as the selected workspace smoke action.

### A/B Decision
- Baseline: a 23-hour scheduler artifact still rendered as `pass`, leaving no operator prompt before the 24-hour freshness gate expired.
- Variant: start warning at `21.6h` while preserving the strict stale threshold at `24.0h`.
- External guidance: GitHub workflow artifacts are run-produced evidence for post-run review, including logs, failures, screenshots, and test results.
- Decision: adopted because operators need advance notice to refresh scheduled evidence before it becomes stale.

### QC Checks
- Manual Playwright probe against `http://127.0.0.1:8172/`
  - Result: `Scheduler age` rendered `23.2h`, `state: warn`, and `Run scheduler refresh soon to avoid stale evidence.`
- `/api/operator/readiness`
  - Result: `freshness.state` stayed `fresh`, `freshness.near_stale` was `true`, and `scheduler_freshness` warning included the scheduler refresh command.
- `python -m py_compile dashboard.py scripts\browser_smoke.py tests\test_dashboard.py tests\test_browser_smoke.py`
  - Result: PASS.
- `python -m pytest tests/test_dashboard.py -k "workspace_smoke_prefers or scheduler" -q`
  - Result: `5 passed, 73 deselected`.
- `python -m pytest tests/test_browser_smoke.py -k "readiness_artifact_copy_actions" -q`
  - Result: `1 passed, 38 deselected`; source guard requires `and not workspace_smoke_uses_launch_final`.
- `python -m pytest tests/test_dashboard.py tests/test_browser_smoke.py -q`
  - Result: `117 passed`.
- `python scripts\browser_smoke.py --port 8173 --timeout 45 --report logs\smoke\dashboard_browser_scheduler_near_stale_warning_2026-06-07.json --screenshot logs\smoke\dashboard_browser_scheduler_near_stale_warning_2026-06-07.png`
  - Result: PASS; `operator_scheduler_age_warns_before_stale` was OK and `operator_artifact_action_manifest` reported `workspace_smoke_uses_launch_final: false`.
- `python scripts\browser_smoke.py --timeout 45`
  - Result: PASS; refreshed `logs\smoke\dashboard_browser_latest.json` with `Scheduler age` at `23.6h`, `state: warn`, and workspace smoke set to `D:\AI project\var\workspace-smoke-getdaytrends-scheduler-near-stale-warning-2026-06-07.json`.
- `python scripts\check_text_hygiene.py`
  - Result: PASS, `0` findings.
- `git diff --check -- dashboard.py scripts\browser_smoke.py tests\test_dashboard.py tests\test_browser_smoke.py`
  - Result: PASS.
- `python ..\..\ops\scripts\run_workspace_smoke.py --scope getdaytrends --json-out ..\..\var\workspace-smoke-getdaytrends-scheduler-near-stale-warning-2026-06-07.json`
  - Result: expected exit `1`; `5/6 PASS`, only `getdaytrends launch readiness gate` failed.

### Remaining Blockers
- CLI/readiness still reports one runtime fallback signal: `database.sqlite_fallback`.
- Live DB doctor still reaches DNS and TCP but fails PostgreSQL with `tenant/user *** not found`.

## 2026-06-07 - Dashboard DB Failure Cache

### Scope
- Added a short process-local DB connection failure cache for dashboard fallback endpoints.
- Limited the cache to `_get_conn()` failures so endpoint handler failures still run and log independently.
- Sanitized cached failure text before reusing it in logs.
- Lowered repeated cache-hit fallback logs from warning stack traces to info-level status.

### A/B Decision
- Baseline: each DB-backed endpoint retried the same failed Supabase pooler connection during one page load, producing repeated degraded traces while the external credential blocker was unchanged.
- Variant: keep the first connection failure as the diagnostic warning, then serve same-process fallback responses from a 30s cache keyed by `DATABASE_URL` and active `_get_conn`.
- External guidance: Python logging guidance distinguishes ordinary status messages from warnings; repeated cache-hit fallback is operational status after the first diagnostic warning.
- Decision: adopted because the dashboard remains explicit about degraded data while avoiding repeated connection attempts and warning noise.

### QC Checks
- `python -m pytest tests/test_dashboard.py::TestDashboardGracefulDegradation::test_db_connection_failure_cache_skips_repeated_connection_attempts -q`
  - Result: `1 passed`; two DB-backed endpoint requests called `_get_conn()` once and cached text stayed secret-masked.
- `python -m pytest tests/test_dashboard.py::TestDashboardGracefulDegradation -q`
  - Result: `16 passed`.
- `python -m pytest tests/test_dashboard.py -k "index_returns_html or graceful or scheduler or workspace_smoke" -q`
  - Result: `26 passed, 53 deselected`.
- `python scripts\browser_smoke.py --port 8189 --timeout 45 --report logs\smoke\dashboard_browser_db_failure_cache_info_2026-06-07.json --screenshot logs\smoke\dashboard_browser_db_failure_cache_info_2026-06-07.png`
  - Result: PASS, `83/83`.
  - Server stderr tail: one `Dashboard endpoint degraded` warning, zero `PostgreSQL connection failed` fallback warnings, and no repeated cached warning lines in stderr.
- Direct browser check against `http://127.0.0.1:8190/`
  - Result: fallback chips rendered 12 endpoint labels with `duplicateChips: []`.
  - Result: fallback copy text had 15 lines, 12 endpoint lines, distinct TAP alert labels, and the readiness refresh command still included `--fail-on-runtime-fallback --require-live-db`.

### Remaining Blockers
- CLI/readiness still reports one runtime fallback signal: `database.sqlite_fallback`.
- Live DB doctor still reaches DNS and TCP but fails PostgreSQL with `tenant/user *** not found`.

## 2026-06-07 - Pytest Deepeval Drift Guard

### Scope
- Diagnosed the repeated post-pytest source drift that restored the stale `workspace-smoke-getdaytrends-launch-final.json` selector branch and matching stale source guards.
- Added `addopts = -p no:deepeval` to `pytest.ini` so local pytest runs do not auto-load the Deepeval pytest plugin path.
- Restored the runtime-proof workspace smoke selector and the browser smoke manifest guard after the drift.

### Diagnosis
- `py_compile` and `scripts\check_text_hygiene.py` did not rewrite source.
- File-hash watcher runs showed the source changing during normal pytest execution with the auto-loaded plugin set.
- `pytest --trace-config` showed `deepeval.plugins.plugin` was auto-loaded and printed `Running teardown with pytest sessionfinish...`.
- Running the same browser smoke subset with `PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1` produced no hash changes.
- Running it with only `-p no:deepeval` also produced no hash changes.

### QC Checks
- `PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1 python -m pytest tests/test_browser_smoke.py -k "fallback_banner_details or readiness_artifact_copy_actions" -q`
  - Result: `2 passed, 37 deselected`; no watcher hash changes.
- `python -m pytest -p no:deepeval tests/test_browser_smoke.py -k "fallback_banner_details or readiness_artifact_copy_actions" -q`
  - Result: `2 passed, 37 deselected`; no watcher hash changes.
- `python -m pytest tests/test_dashboard.py::TestDashboardEnhancements::test_operator_workspace_smoke_prefers_newer_runtime_proof_over_launch_final tests/test_dashboard.py -k "index_returns_html" -q`
  - Result after `pytest.ini` update: `1 passed, 78 deselected`.
- `python -m pytest tests/test_browser_smoke.py -k "fallback_banner_details or readiness_artifact_copy_actions" -q`
  - Result after `pytest.ini` update: `2 passed, 37 deselected`.
- `python -m pytest tests/test_dashboard.py -k "index_returns_html or workspace_smoke" -q`
  - Result: `8 passed, 70 deselected`.
- `python -m pytest tests/test_browser_smoke.py -q`
  - Result: `39 passed`.
- `rg -n "canonical_path|launch_final_path|prefers_launch_final_over|prefers_complete_launch_final_over|and workspace_smoke_uses_launch_final" dashboard.py tests\test_dashboard.py scripts\browser_smoke.py tests\test_browser_smoke.py`
  - Result: no matches.

### Scope
- Made repeated posting-blocker action buttons unique in the accessibility tree while keeping compact visible text.
- Added `aria-labelledby` wiring from each blocker-row button to its visible button text and blocker title.
- Added browser smoke coverage for duplicate computed operator action names.
- Fixed stale Scheduler age card guidance so evidence older than 24 hours says to refresh now.

### A/B Decision
- Baseline: repeated names like `Copy remediation action`, `View recovery packet`, and `Copy recovery bundle` appeared across multiple blocker rows.
- Variant: keep the visible button text unchanged but compute contextual names such as `Copy recovery bundle Blocker: Live DB doctor (live_db_doctor)`.
- External guidance: W3C WCAG 2.2 SC 2.4.6 says headings and labels should describe topic or purpose.
- Decision: adopted because row-level recovery controls are now distinguishable without making the visual layout noisier.

### QC Checks
- Manual Playwright snapshot against `http://127.0.0.1:8184/`
  - Result: blocker controls exposed contextual names, including `Copy recovery bundle Blocker: Live DB doctor (live_db_doctor)`.
- Manual DOM check against `http://127.0.0.1:8184/`
  - Result: `actionCount: 13`, `duplicateActionNames: []`, and `missingActionTitles: []`.
  - Result: Scheduler age was `24.4h`, `state: warn`, with detail `Run scheduler refresh now to restore fresh evidence.`
- `python scripts\browser_smoke.py --port 8185 --timeout 45 --report logs\smoke\dashboard_browser_operator_button_unique_names_2026-06-07.json --screenshot logs\smoke\dashboard_browser_operator_button_unique_names_2026-06-07.png`
  - Result: PASS; `operator_action_button_unique_names` and `operator_scheduler_age_warns_before_stale` were OK.
- `python -m pytest tests/test_dashboard.py -k "index_returns_html or scheduler or workspace_smoke" -q`
  - Result: `10 passed, 68 deselected`.
- `python -m pytest tests/test_browser_smoke.py -q`
  - Result: `39 passed`.
- `python -m py_compile dashboard.py dashboard_html.py scripts\browser_smoke.py tests\test_dashboard.py tests\test_browser_smoke.py`
  - Result: PASS.
- `python scripts\check_text_hygiene.py`
  - Result: PASS, `0` findings.

### Remaining Blockers
- CLI/readiness still reports one runtime fallback signal: `database.sqlite_fallback`.
- Live DB doctor still reaches DNS and TCP but fails PostgreSQL with `tenant/user *** not found`.

## 2026-06-07 - Launch Secret Scan Safe Preview

### Scope
- Added a sanitized dashboard preview for the final getdaytrends launch secret scan artifact.
- Added `GET /api/operator/launch-secret-scan` with safe path validation restricted to workspace `var/getdaytrends-launch-secret-scan*.json` artifacts.
- Rendered only status, timestamps, counts, pattern names, missing paths, scanned samples, and refresh command. Raw scan JSON and finding values are not returned.
- Added `Copy launch secret scan summary` and `Copy launch secret scan preview refresh command` controls.
- Added a regression guard for escaped `\\n` in the launch scan summary builder after direct browser validation found a JavaScript parse error from a literal newline.
- Added browser-smoke source coverage for the new preview and patched the late TAP checkout return-notice block to record timeout state instead of crashing the whole smoke script.

### A/B Decision
- Baseline: copy the scan path and refresh command only, requiring manual JSON inspection.
- Variant: show a safe summary preview in-app and keep raw secret-bearing payloads out of the rendered surface.
- Decision: adopted. External guidance from OWASP Logging Cheat Sheet says tokens, database connection strings, encryption keys, and primary secrets should not be recorded directly and should be removed, masked, sanitized, hashed, or encrypted.

### QC Checks
- Direct Playwright against `http://127.0.0.1:8211/`
  - Result: dashboard loaded, `View launch secret scan` rendered, preview showed `Launch secret scan: valid`, `Current artifacts: included`, and `Summary: 18 scanned, 0 findings, 0 missing`.
  - Result: preview had no raw Postgres URL, tenant/user value, OpenAI-style key, or Google API key matches.
  - Result: `Copy launch secret scan summary` updated the live status region to `Launch secret scan summary copied.` and the copied payload stayed sanitized.
- `node -e "...new vm.Script(...)"` against served dashboard HTML
  - Result: `{"scripts":2,"failures":0}`.
- `python -m py_compile dashboard.py dashboard_html.py scripts\browser_smoke.py tests\test_dashboard.py tests\test_browser_smoke.py`
  - Result: PASS.
- `python -m pytest tests/test_dashboard.py::TestExistingEndpoints::test_index_returns_html tests/test_dashboard.py::TestDashboardEnhancements::test_operator_readiness_endpoint_summarizes_local_artifacts tests/test_dashboard.py::TestDashboardEnhancements::test_operator_launch_secret_scan_endpoint_reads_safe_summary tests/test_browser_smoke.py::test_browser_smoke_covers_readiness_artifact_copy_actions tests/test_browser_smoke.py::test_tap_checkout_return_smoke_script_covers_success_cancel_and_clear -q`
  - Result: `5 passed`.
- `python scripts\check_text_hygiene.py --file ..\..\docs\reports\2026-06\AUTO_RESEARCH_GETDAYTRENDS_LAUNCH_SECRET_SCAN_SAFE_PREVIEW_2026-06-07.md --report logs\hygiene\text_hygiene_launch_secret_scan_safe_preview_report_2026-06-07.json`
  - Result: PASS, `checked=1`, `findings=0`, `read_errors=0`.
- `python scripts\check_text_hygiene.py`
  - Result: PASS, `checked=6`, `findings=0`, `read_errors=0`.
- `python ops\scripts\getdaytrends_launch_secret_scan.py --include-current-artifacts --json-out var\getdaytrends-launch-secret-scan-final-2026-06-07.json`
  - Result: `status=valid`, `scanned=18`, `findings=0`, `missing=0`.

### Full Smoke Note
- Full `scripts\browser_smoke.py` remains unstable in this cycle. It previously reached or passed the new launch secret scan preview path, then timed out later around TAP checkout return-notice behavior or hung without writing a final report. Keep this as follow-up instead of treating the full smoke as passed.

### Remaining Blockers
- CLI/readiness still reports runtime fallback `database.sqlite_fallback`.
- Provider auth remains externally blocked by leaked-key or permission state.
- Live DB doctor still reaches DNS/TCP but fails PostgreSQL with masked `tenant/user *** not found`.

- Baseline: full smoke could fail or hang around launch scan preview/TAP follow-up paths because it gated on unstable clipboard reads and exact age labels.
- Variant: verify the real user click, copied feedback, sanitized payload, and stable manifest semantics; keep unstable values as diagnostics.

## 2026-06-09 - AutoResearch Release Evidence Bootstrap Consistency Guard

### Scope
- Added a bootstrap-only missing-consistency allowance to `refresh_complete_goal_release_manifest.py`.
- Updated `complete_goal_release_evidence_refresh.py` so only the two manifest refreshes before evidence consistency pass `--allow-missing-consistency`; the final manifest refresh remains strict.
- Updated `complete_goal_no_credential_refresh.py` so an externally blocked child release-evidence report still fails local unless `summary.release_approval.generated_after_final_manifest_refresh` is `true`.
- Added regression coverage for the allowed bootstrap miss, ordered manifest-refresh arguments, and stale child approval freshness rejection.

### A/B Decision
- Baseline: the 2026-06-09 date rollover exposed a circular bootstrap path where the first release manifest refresh required `complete-goal-evidence-consistency-current-2026-06-09.json` before the release-evidence driver had reached the consistency step that creates it.
- Variant: allow that single missing source only during pre-consistency manifest bootstrap while preserving strict final manifest and release approval freshness checks.
- Decision: adopted. This removes a local date-boundary ordering failure without weakening the final release gate.

## 2026-06-08 - AutoResearch Launch Scan Recovery Packet Contract Gate

### Scope
- Promoted the Supabase recovery packet accepted-shape contract from `scripts\verify_supabase_recovery_packet.py` into the current getdaytrends launch secret scan gate.
- `ops\scripts\getdaytrends_launch_secret_scan.py --include-current-artifacts` now validates that `logs\readiness\supabase_recovery_packet_latest.json` includes both official Transaction pooler shapes, the shared/dedicated acceptance booleans, and secret-hygiene flags.
- `ops\scripts\complete_goal_release_evidence_refresh.py` now exposes the getdaytrends-specific `supabase_recovery_packet_contract_ok` summary field, so release evidence no longer has to infer packet coverage from secret-scan cleanliness alone.

### A/B Decision
- Baseline: getdaytrends launch secret scan checked value-shaped secrets in the current recovery packet but did not fail if the accepted pooler-shape contract drifted; release evidence showed only the DailyNews repair-packet contract signal.
- Variant: keep the scan's existing secret checks and add a current-artifact-only recovery-packet contract validator for the two passwordless accepted shapes.
- Decision: adopted. The variant fails closed on missing dedicated PgBouncer shape metadata while keeping the current clean artifact valid.

### External Check
- Supabase's current database connection docs list Shared Pooler transaction mode on `aws-[region].pooler.supabase.com:6543` and Dedicated Pooler transaction mode on `db.[project-id].supabase.co:6543`.
- Reference: https://supabase.com/docs/guides/database/connecting-to-postgres

### QC Checks
- `python -m pytest tests\test_getdaytrends_launch_secret_scan.py tests\test_complete_goal_release_evidence_refresh.py -q -p no:cacheprovider`
  - Result: `12 passed`.

- `python -m py_compile ops\scripts\getdaytrends_launch_secret_scan.py ops\scripts\complete_goal_release_evidence_refresh.py tests\test_getdaytrends_launch_secret_scan.py tests\test_complete_goal_release_evidence_refresh.py`

## 2026-06-08 - AutoResearch TAP Reset Presets Clears Filter

### Scope
- Fixed the TAP `Reset presets` flow that restored the default preset list but left the previous custom target market filter active.
- Changed `resetTapPresets` to restore default presets, clear the target-market input, sync the TAP views back to the all-market queue, and toast `Preset markets reset and filter cleared.`
- Added `tap_reset_presets_clears_filter` to dashboard browser smoke so reset must leave `ALL` active, target blank, default queue status, and no stale market suffix.

### A/B Decision
- Baseline: direct Playwright click-through saved CANADA, then `Reset presets` removed CANADA from the preset list while the target input and queue status still showed CANADA.
- Variant: reset presets as a full default-state reset for the preset filter surface.
- Decision: adopted. Direct Playwright on `http://127.0.0.1:8070/` reported target blank, `ALL` active, status `0 queued alert(s) loaded.`, toast `Preset markets reset and filter cleared.`, and `aria-busy=false`.

### External Check
- Carbon filtering guidance says clearing filters returns filters to their original default starting state.
- Carbon disclosure guidance says settings/filter controls can use buttons to reset or apply selected changes.
- Reference: https://carbondesignsystem.com/patterns/filtering/
- Reference: https://carbondesignsystem.com/patterns/disclosures-pattern/

### QC Checks
- `python -m py_compile dashboard_html.py scripts\browser_smoke.py`
  - Result: PASS.

- Direct Playwright variant check on `http://127.0.0.1:8070/`
  - Result: after reset, target blank, `ALL` active, status `0 queued alert(s) loaded.`, toast `Preset markets reset and filter cleared.`, `aria-busy=false`.

## 2026-06-08 - AutoResearch TAP Save Preset Applies Filter

### Scope
- Fixed the TAP target preset flow where `Save preset` marked the new market preset as active while the queue status still reflected the previously loaded target.
- Changed `saveCurrentTapPreset` to save the preset, render the active preset state, sync the TAP operator views for that target, and then toast `Saved and applied preset for MARKET.`
- Strengthened `tap_preset_pressed_state` browser smoke so it now requires the active preset, queue status, and toast to agree on the same target market.

### A/B Decision
- Baseline: direct Playwright click-through with `canada` showed CANADA as the pressed preset, but the queue status did not include `for CANADA`.
- Variant: after saving the preset, immediately run the same TAP sync path used by applying a preset or pressing Enter.
- Decision: adopted. Direct Playwright on `http://127.0.0.1:8069/` reported CANADA pressed, status `0 queued alert(s) loaded for CANADA.`, toast `Saved and applied preset for CANADA.`, and `aria-busy=false`.

### External Check
- Carbon filtering guidance says users need visible filter state and a way to clear or reset filters to the default state.
- Carbon disclosure guidance says settings/filter controls should keep actions clear and use buttons to reset or apply changes.
- Reference: https://carbondesignsystem.com/patterns/filtering/
- Reference: https://carbondesignsystem.com/patterns/disclosures-pattern/

## 2026-06-08 - AutoResearch TAP Empty Dispatch Feedback

### Scope
- Fixed the TAP alert queue empty-dispatch feedback that reported `Dispatch finished: 0 sent, 0 failed.` even though no delivery attempt occurred.
- Added `tapDispatchResultMessage` so dry runs, empty dispatches, and non-empty dispatch outcomes use distinct operator-facing status messages.
- Extended dashboard browser smoke with `tap_empty_dispatch_feedback`, using an empty target-market filter and verifying status, toast, outcomes, and `aria-busy` state.

### A/B Decision
- Baseline: direct Playwright click-through showed an empty queued-alert dispatch ending with `Dispatch finished: 0 sent, 0 failed.` while the outcomes panel still said `No dispatch attempts yet`.
- Variant: treat `dispatched=0` and `failed=0` as a no-op empty state with `No queued TAP alerts to dispatch.`, while keeping non-empty dispatch summaries unchanged.
- Decision: adopted. Direct Playwright on `http://127.0.0.1:8068/` reported status and toast `No queued TAP alerts to dispatch.`, outcomes `No dispatch attempts yet`, `badFinishedCopy=false`, and `aria-busy=false`.

### External Check
- W3C WCAG status-message guidance covers programmatic status updates that inform users of action results without moving focus.
- Reference: https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG22/Understanding/status-messages.html

- `python -m py_compile dashboard_html.py scripts\browser_smoke.py`

## 2026-06-08 - AutoResearch TAP All-States Empty Copy

### Scope
- Fixed the TAP alert queue `All states` empty-state copy that rendered as `No all alerts`.
- Fixed the matching live-region status that rendered as `0 all alert(s) loaded.`
- Added a real browser smoke interaction that selects `All states` and rejects the bad grammar while allowing an active market suffix.

### A/B Decision
- Baseline: direct Playwright selection of `Lifecycle -> All states` showed `No all alerts` and `0 all alert(s) loaded.`
- Variant: use lifecycle display helpers so the all-state case reads `No alerts` / `0 alert(s) loaded`, while specific states keep `No queued alerts` and market filters can append `for <MARKET>`.
- Decision: adopted. Direct Playwright reported `No alerts`, `0 alert(s) loaded.`, and `badCopyPresent=false`; full smoke reported `No alerts for UNITED-STATES` when the prior market preset was active.

### External Check
- Carbon Design System empty-state guidance recommends contextual, concise empty states that explain what will appear and guide the next step.
- Stack Overflow Design System notes empty states should orient the user and explain next steps when a search/filter yields no results.
- Reference: https://carbondesignsystem.com/patterns/empty-states-pattern
- Reference: https://stackoverflow.design/system/components/empty-states

### QC Checks

## 2026-06-08 - AutoResearch TAP Alert Queue Filter Labels

### Scope
- Replaced implicit TAP alert queue filter labels with explicit `label for` associations and `aria-labelledby` idrefs for `Target market`, `Lifecycle`, and `Batch size`.
- Added a named `role="group"` container for the TAP alert queue filters.
- Extended browser smoke to verify filter group label, control order, visible label text, native label association, idref text, and 28px+ target height.

### A/B Decision
- Baseline: direct Playwright inspection found the three visible TAP filter controls were reachable but had no explicit label/idref contract in the DOM extraction, and the filter group itself was unnamed.
- Variant: preserve the visible labels while making each form control explicitly associated by `for`/`id` and `aria-labelledby`, then fail browser smoke if labels, order, or target height regress.
- Decision: adopted. Exact Playwright role/name selectors can now fill `Target market`, select `Lifecycle`, and select `Batch size`; the live status updated to `0 failed alert(s) loaded for KOREA.`.

### External Check
- W3C WAI Labeling Controls recommends visible labels for all form controls and explicit `label for` associations when possible.
- W3C WCAG 2.2 SC 3.3.2 says labels or instructions are required when content accepts user input.
- MDN text-label guidance covers labels/names for `input` and `select` controls.

### QC Checks
- Direct Playwright baseline and variant click-through on `http://127.0.0.1:8063/`
  - Result: exact role/name targeting worked for `Target market`, `Lifecycle`, and `Batch size`.
  - Detail: filter group `TAP alert queue filters`; controls associated with `Target market`, `Lifecycle`, and `Batch size`; control heights `36`, `36`, `36`; status `0 failed alert(s) loaded for KOREA.`.
- `python -m pytest tests/test_dashboard.py::TestExistingEndpoints::test_index_returns_html tests/test_browser_smoke.py::test_browser_smoke_covers_tap_alert_queue_filter_labels -q`

## 2026-06-08 - AutoResearch TAP Alert Queue Action Groups

### Scope
- Added named `role="group"` containers for TAP target-market preset actions, dynamic preset options, and alert queue dispatch actions.
- Added explicit accessible names for preset buttons, `Save preset`, `Reset presets`, `Dispatch queued`, `Dry run`, and `Refresh queue`.
- Extended real browser smoke to verify group labels, button order, aria labels, explicit button type, and rendered target height after exercising preset save and target-market Enter workflows.

### A/B Decision
- Baseline: direct Playwright inspection showed TAP preset and queue action controls were reachable and had 30px+ target sizes, but their clusters had no group role/label and the primary action buttons had no explicit accessible names.
- Variant: make the preset/action clusters named groups and fail browser smoke if the dynamic preset labels, queue action order, button types, or 28px target-height contract regress.
- Decision: adopted. The live dashboard smoke now includes `tap_alert_queue_action_groups` and reports no gaps.

### External Check
- MDN ARIA `group` role guidance and W3C WCAG target-size guidance remain the adopted references for named related-control clusters and minimum control target size.

### QC Checks
- Direct Playwright inspection of `http://127.0.0.1:8062/`
  - Result: baseline TAP preset/action rows had empty role/label values; buttons clicked cleanly, and `Dry run` updated status to `Dry run checked 0 alert(s).`.
- `python -m pytest tests/test_dashboard.py::TestExistingEndpoints::test_index_returns_html tests/test_browser_smoke.py::test_browser_smoke_covers_tap_alert_queue_action_groups -q`
  - Result: `2 passed`.
- `python -m py_compile dashboard_html.py scripts\browser_smoke.py`
  - Result: PASS.
- `python scripts\browser_smoke.py --timeout 45 --report logs\smoke\dashboard_browser_tap_alert_action_groups_2026-06-08.json --screenshot logs\smoke\dashboard_browser_tap_alert_action_groups_2026-06-08.png`
  - Result: PASS, `100/100`.
  - TAP action group detail: preset action min height `31`, alert action min height `36`, `gaps=[]`.
- `python -m pytest tests/test_dashboard.py tests/test_browser_smoke.py -q`
  - Result: `125 passed`.

## 2026-06-08 - AutoResearch Log Mojibake Compatibility Guard

### Scope
- Tightened dashboard log sanitization for actual CJK Compatibility Ideograph mojibake (`U+F900`-`U+FAFF`) without hiding valid UTF-8 Korean log lines.
- Extended browser smoke mojibake detection to fail if compatibility-ideograph garble reaches the visible pipeline log panel.
- Confirmed by raw Python code-point inspection that the current browser log artifact contains valid Hangul and zero compatibility-ideograph characters even though PowerShell renders it poorly.

### A/B Decision
- Baseline: the log panel smoke checked C1 controls, replacement characters, and question/Hangul pairs, but did not explicitly cover compatibility-ideograph mojibake patterns.
- Variant: add a focused compatibility-ideograph detector to both the API sanitizer and browser smoke, with tests using ASCII `\u` escapes so fixtures are encoding-stable on Windows.
- Decision: adopted. The real dashboard smoke now passes the stricter visible-log contract, and the API test hides actual compatibility-ideograph mojibake while preserving valid Korean evidence.

### External Check
- Unicode charts list `F900`-`FAFF` as the CJK Compatibility Ideographs block, matching the code-point range used by the new guard.

### QC Checks
- `python -m pytest tests/test_dashboard.py -k "logs_endpoint_sanitizes" -q`
  - Result: `1 passed`, `82 deselected`.
- `python -m pytest tests/test_browser_smoke.py::test_mojibake_marker_set_covers_manual_dashboard_log_garble tests/test_browser_smoke.py::test_browser_smoke_covers_log_viewer_live_region -q`
  - Result: `2 passed`.
- `python scripts\browser_smoke.py --timeout 45 --report logs\smoke\dashboard_browser_log_mojibake_compat_guard_2026-06-08.json --screenshot logs\smoke\dashboard_browser_log_mojibake_compat_guard_2026-06-08.png`
  - Result: PASS, `99/99`; `dashboard_log_viewer_has_no_mojibake_markers` passed with an empty marker list.
- `python -m pytest tests/test_dashboard.py tests/test_browser_smoke.py -q`
  - Result: `124 passed`.
- `python -m py_compile dashboard.py scripts\browser_smoke.py`
  - Result: PASS.
- `python -m pytest tests\test_readiness_check.py tests\test_verify_supabase_recovery_packet.py tests\test_dashboard.py tests\test_browser_smoke.py -q`

  - Result: PASS; packet clear.

## 2026-06-08 - AutoResearch TAP Offer Action Group

### Scope
- Wrapped TAP deal-room offer actions in labeled `role="group"` containers.
- Added offer-specific accessible names for `Track click` and checkout buttons.
- Extended TAP fixture browser smoke to verify the seeded offer action group, action order, button aria labels, explicit button type, and rendered target height.

### A/B Decision
- Baseline: TAP offer cards had working buttons, but the `Track click` and checkout controls were a plain flex row with generic button names.
- Variant: group the offer controls as `<offer> offer actions`, name each button with the offer headline, and fail TAP fixture smoke if order, type, labels, or 28px target height regress.
- Decision: adopted. The fixture browser run clicked the offer, exercised checkout recovery, and reported one clean offer action group with no gaps.

## 2026-06-08 - AutoResearch Fallback Summary Target Size

### Scope
- Raised the fallback warning disclosure summary target from a rendered 14px height to a 28px target while preserving the native `<details>/<summary>` disclosure.
- Extended `dashboard_fallback_banner_details` browser smoke to record `summaryHeight`/`summaryWidth` and fail if the fallback summary target drops below 28px.
- Source-gated the dashboard CSS and browser smoke contract so the degraded-endpoint disclosure stays easy to activate.

### A/B Decision
- Baseline: live Playwright inspection found the `View degraded endpoint details` summary as the only remaining dashboard interactive target below the WCAG 2.2 target-size floor, measuring 14px high.
- Variant: keep the native summary label and disclosure behavior, add a 28px minimum target via compact padding/line-height, and verify the rendered target in Chromium.
- Decision: adopted. Direct Playwright measured the patched summary at `height=28`, `width=1169`, and confirmed the details panel toggled open.

### External Check
- W3C WCAG 2.2 SC 2.5.8 defines a target-size floor of at least 24 by 24 CSS pixels, with spacing exceptions.
- MDN documents `<summary>` as the native disclosure summary element used with `<details>`, so the implementation keeps that semantic control instead of replacing it with a custom button.
- Reference: https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG22/Understanding/target-size-minimum.html
- Reference: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/summary

### QC Checks
- `python -m pytest tests/test_dashboard.py::TestExistingEndpoints::test_index_returns_html tests/test_browser_smoke.py::test_browser_smoke_covers_fallback_banner_details -q`

- `python -m py_compile dashboard_html.py scripts\browser_smoke.py`

  - Result: summary text `View degraded endpoint details`, `height=28`, native details toggled open.

## 2026-06-08 - AutoResearch Reused Packet Label Spacing

### Scope
- Fixed the mobile posting-blocker recovery title that rendered as `Recovery packetSame packet as cli_smoke_report`.
- Reused the existing `.operator-action-title` flex/gap pattern and inserted a literal text separator before the reused-packet note.
- Extended `operator_recovery_row_action_group` browser smoke to capture recovery title text and fail if reused-packet labels become concatenated again.

### A/B Decision
- Baseline: live mobile Playwright inspection showed the second posting blocker rendered `Recovery packetSame packet as cli_smoke_report`, making the reused recovery-packet cue harder to scan.
- Variant: render the recovery title and note as an action-title group with readable spacing while leaving button order, target size, and packet actions unchanged.
- Decision: adopted. Mobile Playwright measured the patched row title as `Recovery packet Same packet as cli_smoke_report`, with no action-order changes.

### External Check
- MDN documents `gap` as the CSS shorthand for spacing rows and columns in flex, grid, and multi-column containers.
- W3C WCAG labels/instructions guidance emphasizes enough visible cues for users to accomplish the task without confusion.
- Reference: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/gap
- Reference: https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG22/Understanding/labels-or-instructions.html
